page: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz with 2 physical cores and 4 dedicated vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled with a 614 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance asymmetry between single-core and multi-core operations. The instance achieves average performance in single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU testing and Geekbench single-core evaluations. However, multi-core performance is categorized as weak, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also weak, though the Passmark memory score remains in the average tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers high memory density relative to its compute allocation, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. The high RAM-to-vCPU ratio is suitable for workloads requiring large memory footprints but modest parallel processing power. Recommended workloads include in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from the 128.0 GB RAM and 614 GB local storage without demanding high multi-threaded CPU throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance from the EDSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 614 GB local disk. While single-core CPU and Passmark memory benchmarks perform in the average tier, multi-core CPU capabilities and uncached memory bandwidth are weak. This hardware profile presents a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not require dense parallel computing. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity alongside moderate compute resources.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high RAM density and local storage for memory-intensive workloads with moderate parallel processing needs.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
